extension.valueMarkdown:
## Character
62-year-old Nigerian-American woman, retired high school biology teacher. Lives in Houston, TX with her husband Emeka. Non-smoker, but 15-year history of secondhand smoke exposure (husband smoked until 2015). Active church member, walks 2 miles daily. Insurance: Medicare (age 62 with disability determination due to cancer diagnosis).

## Clinical Scenario
Patricia was diagnosed with stage IIIA non-small cell lung cancer (adenocarcinoma) in October 2024 after presenting with persistent cough and unintentional weight loss. Her PCP ordered a chest CT showing a 4.2 cm right upper lobe mass with ipsilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy. She was referred to MD Anderson Cancer Center for staging workup: PET/CT, brain MRI, and endobronchial ultrasound-guided biopsy (EBUS) confirming adenocarcinoma with PD-L1 TPS 60% and no actionable driver mutations (EGFR wild-type, ALK/ROS1 negative).

She was enrolled in a Phase III clinical trial (NCT05820295 — a real-ish trial ID) comparing pembrolizumab + carboplatin/pemetrexed vs standard chemo alone for first-line treatment of stage III NSCLC. She signed informed consent in December 2024 and began treatment in January 2025. She has completed 4 cycles of chemo-immunotherapy with partial response on interim imaging. She continues maintenance pembrolizumab with regular monitoring.

The research institute (MD Anderson) uses a Permission Ticket to access Patricia's historical medical records from her community PCP and pulmonologist, covering the 3 years prior to enrollment through the present, to correlate treatment response with prior health status.

## Sites
- **Westheimer Family Medicine** — PCP in Houston, TX (2018–present). Annual wellness visits, initial workup of cough/weight loss, chronic disease management (hypertension, pre-diabetes).
- **Houston Pulmonary Associates** — Pulmonologist in Houston, TX (2024). Initial chest CT referral, pulmonary function testing, referred to MD Anderson.
- **MD Anderson Cancer Center** — Oncology in Houston, TX (2024–present). Staging, biopsy, clinical trial enrollment, chemo-immunotherapy, monitoring.

Three sites, one state (TX). Tests Permission Ticket constraints on **periods** (research needs 2022–present, not her full medical history) and **scopes** (research needs Condition, Observation, DiagnosticReport, MedicationRequest, Procedure — broad but defined by study protocol).

code.text: Right upper lobe lung mass, suspicious for malignancy

coding.display: Structure of upper lobe of right lung (body structure)

note.text: 4.2 cm spiculated mass with ipsilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy (stations 4R and 7). Clinical stage at minimum IIIA (T2b N2). Tissue diagnosis pending. First identified on chest X-ray August 1, 2024; CT characterization August 8, 2024.
Resolved: Confirmed as primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma by EBUS-TBNA tissue diagnosis on 2024-10-02. See Condition/enc-013-adenocarcinoma for definitive diagnosis.

code.text: Non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma subtype, right upper lobe primary

coding.display: Structure of upper lobe of right lung (body structure)
American Joint Committee on Cancer stage IIIA (qualifier value)

note.text: Final staging: Stage IIIA (T2b N2 M0). Molecular profiling (finalized 2024-10-10): KRAS G12C positive, EGFR wild-type, ALK rearrangement negative, ROS1 rearrangement negative, BRAF V600E negative, MET amplification negative. PD-L1 TPS 60% (high expression) by 22C3 IHC. No actionable driver mutations for first-line targeted therapy. Unresectable disease given N2 nodal involvement. Brain MRI (2024-09-25): no intracranial metastases.
Adenocarcinoma confirmed in both station 4R (right lower paratracheal) and station 7 (subcarinal) lymph nodes, establishing N2 disease.

Grade 1 per CTCAE v5.0. Bilateral fingertip numbness and tingling, no motor component. Attributed to cumulative carboplatin toxicity. Gabapentin 300 mg PO at bedtime initiated for symptomatic management.

2025-03-10 (Cycle 4): Improved from grade 2 at Cycle 3 to grade 1 on gabapentin 300 mg QHS. Numbness now limited to fingertip tips bilaterally, not interfering with daily activities. Carboplatin dose reduction from Cycle 3 maintained.

coding.display: Fatigue associated with malignant neoplastic disease (finding)

note.text: Worsened from CTCAE grade 1 at Cycle 2 to grade 2 at Cycle 3. Now limiting instrumental ADLs — reduced walking distance, daily napping. Contributed to carboplatin dose reduction decision.
2025-03-10 (Cycle 4): Improved to CTCAE grade 1, more manageable. Patient has resumed walking one mile daily.

conclusionCode.text: FDG-avid right upper lobe mass highly suspicious for primary lung malignancy

resource.conclusion: Metabolically active right upper lobe mass (4.3 x 3.8 cm, SUVmax 12.4) with ipsilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy (station 4R: 2.1 cm, SUVmax 8.1; station 7: 1.5 cm, SUVmax 6.3), consistent with clinical stage III lung malignancy. No distant metastases identified.

performer.display: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center

resource.conclusion: No pneumothorax, no new pleural effusion, stable appearance of known right upper lobe mass.

performer.display: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center

resource.conclusion: Next-generation sequencing panel (finalized 2024-10-10): EGFR wild-type, ALK rearrangement negative, ROS1 rearrangement negative, BRAF V600E negative, KRAS G12C POSITIVE, MET amplification negative. PD-L1 expression by 22C3 immunohistochemistry: Tumor Proportion Score (TPS) 60% (high expression). No actionable driver mutations identified for first-line targeted therapy. KRAS G12C mutation identified.

performer.display: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center

resource.conclusion: Adenocarcinoma identified in both station 4R and station 7 lymph node samples. Immunophenotype (TTF-1+, CK7+, CK20−, p40−) consistent with primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma.

resource.conclusion: Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) 62%, normal wall motion, no valvular abnormalities, no pericardial effusion. Cardiac function adequate for trial enrollment (LVEF >50% required).

conclusionCode.text: RECIST 1.1: Partial response — RUL mass decreased 33% from baseline
Non-small cell lung cancer, stage IIIA, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe

resource.conclusion: Right upper lobe mass decreased from 4.2 cm to 2.8 cm (33% reduction from baseline). RECIST 1.1: Partial response. Interval decrease in right paratracheal (station 4R) and subcarinal (station 7) lymphadenopathy. No new pulmonary nodules or evidence of distant metastatic disease. No hepatic lesions, adrenal abnormalities, or osseous metastases.

coding.display: Transbronchial needle aspiration of mediastinal lymph node using endoscopic endobronchial ultrasonography guidance (procedure)

location.display: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center

bodySite.text: Mediastinal lymph node station 4R (right lower paratracheal)

note.text: Flexible bronchoscope with integrated ultrasound transducer, oral approach, 22-gauge aspiration needle under real-time EBUS visualization. 3 passes at station 4R, 3 passes at station 7. Rapid on-site cytology evaluation (ROSE) confirmed adequate tissue at both sites. Moderate sedation per institutional protocol. Estimated blood loss: minimal.

reasonCode.text: Bilateral knee osteoarthritis

note.text: Pharmacist Dr. Amy Pham flagged potential nephrotoxicity interaction with carboplatin. Patient counseled to minimize NSAID use during chemotherapy and use acetaminophen as first-line analgesic for knee pain. Contact clinic before taking naproxen.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth as needed for knee pain; minimize use during chemotherapy per pharmacy recommendation
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take 1 tablet by mouth as needed for knee pain. Minimize use during chemotherapy. Use acetaminophen as first-line analgesic. Contact clinic before taking naproxen.

reasonCode.text: Required pemetrexed supplementation

reasonReference.display: Non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe

dosageInstruction.text: 1000 mcg intramuscular, administered in office on 2024-12-02

reasonCode.text: Pemetrexed premedication to reduce hypersensitivity reactions and skin toxicity

dosageInstruction.text: Take 4 mg by mouth the day before, day of, and day after pemetrexed infusion per protocol

reasonCode.text: Pemetrexed supplementation (lung cancer treatment protocol)

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 mg by mouth once daily, starting today; must be taken for at least 7 days prior to first pemetrexed dose; continue until directed to stop
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take 1 tablet (1 mg) by mouth once daily. Must be started at least 7 days before first pemetrexed dose. Continue until directed to stop.

reasonCode.text: Prevention of chemotherapy-induced nausea (lung cancer treatment)

reasonReference.display: Non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe

dosageInstruction.text: Take 8 mg by mouth as pre-medication prior to chemotherapy
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take 8 mg by mouth as pre-medication prior to chemotherapy.

statusReason.text: Induction chemotherapy completed per protocol (4 cycles); transitioning to maintenance pembrolizumab monotherapy

medicationCodeableConcept.text: carboplatin AUC 3.75 intravenous infusion (calculated dose 338 mg, reduced 25% from AUC 5)

reasonReference.display: Non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe

dosageInstruction.text: AUC 3.75 IV over 30 minutes, every 3 weeks (dose reduced 25% from AUC 5; calculated 338 mg per Calvert formula with current GFR)
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Carboplatin AUC 3.75 IV infusion over 30 minutes every 3 weeks. Dose reduced from AUC 5 due to cumulative toxicity.

note.text: Cycle 1 administered 2025-01-06. No infusion reactions. Dose calculated via Calvert formula: target AUC 5 x (GFR 68 + 25) = 465, rounded to 450 mg per protocol.
Cycle 3 (2025-02-17): Dose reduced 25% from AUC 5 to AUC 3.75 (338 mg) per protocol guidelines for cumulative toxicity (grade 2 fatigue, grade 1 peripheral neuropathy).

Discontinued 2025-03-24: 4 induction cycles completed per clinical trial NCT05820295 protocol. Interim restaging CT shows partial response (RECIST 1.1). Transitioning to maintenance pembrolizumab monotherapy.

reasonCode.text: Chemotherapy pre-medication per protocol

dosageInstruction.text: 4 mg by mouth, morning of infusion (chemotherapy pre-medication)
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take 4 mg by mouth the morning of infusion.

reasonCode.text: Chemotherapy-induced nausea prophylaxis (pre-medication)

dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Ondansetron 8 mg IV administered prior to chemotherapy.

reasonCode.text: Chemotherapy-induced nausea/vomiting prophylaxis

reasonReference.display: Non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe

dosageInstruction.text: 8 mg by mouth every 8 hours as needed for nausea/vomiting for 5 days
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take 8 mg by mouth every 8 hours as needed for nausea or vomiting for 5 days.

reasonReference.display: Non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma, right upper lobe

dosageInstruction.text: 200 mg IV over 30 minutes, every 3 weeks (maintenance phase per NCT05820295, following completion of 4 induction cycles)
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV infusion over 30 minutes every 3 weeks. Maintenance therapy.

note.text: Cycle 1 administered 2025-01-06. No infusion reactions. Per clinical trial NCT05820295 protocol.

2025-03-24: Transitioned to maintenance monotherapy following completion of 4 induction cycles (pembrolizumab + carboplatin + pemetrexed). Interim restaging CT shows partial response (RECIST 1.1, 33% decrease in target lesion). Carboplatin and pemetrexed discontinued per protocol.

statusReason.text: Induction chemotherapy completed per protocol (4 cycles); transitioning to maintenance pembrolizumab monotherapy

medicationCodeableConcept.text: pemetrexed 500 mg/m² intravenous infusion (BSA 1.72 m², calculated dose 860 mg)

dosageInstruction.text: 500 mg/m² IV over 10 minutes, every 3 weeks (BSA 1.72 m², calculated dose 860 mg)
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Pemetrexed 860 mg IV infusion over 10 minutes every 3 weeks.

note.text: Cycle 1 administered 2025-01-06. No infusion reactions. Patient on folic acid and B12 supplementation per pemetrexed protocol requirements.

Discontinued 2025-03-24: 4 induction cycles completed per clinical trial NCT05820295 protocol. Interim restaging CT shows partial response (RECIST 1.1). Transitioning to maintenance pembrolizumab monotherapy.

dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take 1 capsule (300 mg) by mouth at bedtime. May cause drowsiness; do not drive until tolerance is established.

note.text: New start for chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy management. Dr. Amy Pham provided medication counseling. Dose escalation planned if symptoms persist or worsen.

dosageInstruction.text: AUC 3.75 IV over 30 minutes, Cycle 4 of 4 (final induction cycle); continued at 25% dose reduction from Cycle 3 modification for neuropathy (calculated dose 338 mg per Calvert formula)
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Carboplatin 338 mg IV infusion over 30 minutes. Dose reduced from AUC 5 due to cumulative toxicity.

note.text: Cycle 4 of 4 (final induction cycle) administered 2025-03-10. No infusion reactions. Continued at 25% dose reduction (AUC 3.75, 338 mg) from Cycle 3 modification for cumulative neuropathy. Final carboplatin dose per induction protocol; carboplatin to be discontinued for maintenance phase.
